result.stdout:
Description:
  Motus CLI - Browser automation toolkit

Usage:
  Motus.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  run <assemblies>  Discover and execute tests from assemblies
  record            Record browser interactions and generate test code
  codegen <url>     Generate page object models from live web pages
  install           Download and install a browser for automation
  update-protocol   Download and update CDP protocol definitions
  screenshot <url>  Capture a screenshot of a web page
  pdf <url>         Generate a PDF from a web page
  trace             Trace management commands

commands.payload:
Description:
  Motus CLI - Browser automation toolkit

Usage:
  Motus.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  run <assemblies>  Discover and execute tests from assemblies
  record            Record browser interactions and generate test code
  codegen <url>     Generate page object models from live web pages
  install           Download and install a browser for automation
  update-protocol   Download and update CDP protocol definitions
  screenshot <url>  Capture a screenshot of a web page
  pdf <url>         Generate a PDF from a web page
  trace             Trace management commands

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate page object models from live web pages

Usage:
  Motus.Cli codegen <url>... [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  One or more URLs to generate page objects for

Options:
  --output             Output directory for generated files [default: .]
  --namespace          Namespace for generated classes [default: Motus.Generated]
  --selector-priority  Comma-separated selector strategy priority (e.g. testid,role,text,css)
  --timeout            Navigation timeout in milliseconds [default: 30000]
  --detect-listeners   Detect elements with JS event listeners (for vanilla JS, jQuery, etc.)
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate page object models from live web pages

Usage:
  Motus.Cli codegen <url>... [options]

Arguments:

Options:

  --namespace          Namespace for generated classes [default: Motus.Generated]
  --selector-priority  Comma-separated selector strategy priority (e.g. testid,role,text,css)
  --timeout            Navigation timeout in milliseconds [default: 30000]
  --detect-listeners   Detect elements with JS event listeners (for vanilla JS, jQuery, etc.)
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download and install a browser for automation

Usage:
  Motus.Cli install [options]

Options:
  --channel       Browser channel to install (chromium, chrome, edge, firefox) [default: chromium]
  --revision      Pin to a specific browser revision
  --path          Override browser cache directory
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Download and install a browser for automation

Usage:
  Motus.Cli install [options]

Options:
  --channel       Browser channel to install (chromium, chrome, edge, firefox) [default: chromium]
  --revision      Pin to a specific browser revision
  --path          Override browser cache directory
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate a PDF from a web page

Usage:
  Motus.Cli pdf <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  URL to render as PDF

Options:
  --output        Output file path [default: output.pdf]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  Motus.Cli pdf <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  URL to render as PDF

Options:
  --output        Output file path [default: output.pdf]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Record browser interactions and generate test code

Usage:
  Motus.Cli record [options]

Options:
  --url                Starting URL to navigate to
  --output             Output file path [default: recorded-test.cs]
  --framework          Test framework (mstest, xunit, nunit) [default: mstest]
  --connect            WebSocket endpoint to connect to an existing browser
  --selector-priority  Selector priority strategy (reserved for future use)
  --class-name         Generated test class name [default: RecordedTest]
  --method-name        Generated test method name [default: RecordedScenario]
  --namespace          Generated test namespace [default: Motus.Generated]
  --preserve-timing    Emit delays between actions matching the original user timing
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Record browser interactions and generate test code

Usage:
  Motus.Cli record [options]

Options:
  --url                Starting URL to navigate to
  --output             Output file path [default: recorded-test.cs]
  --framework          Test framework (mstest, xunit, nunit) [default: mstest]
  --connect            WebSocket endpoint to connect to an existing browser
  --selector-priority  Selector priority strategy (reserved for future use)
  --class-name         Generated test class name [default: RecordedTest]
  --method-name        Generated test method name [default: RecordedScenario]
  --namespace          Generated test namespace [default: Motus.Generated]
  --preserve-timing    Emit delays between actions matching the original user timing
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Discover and execute tests from assemblies

Usage:
  Motus.Cli run [<assemblies>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <assemblies>  Test assembly paths

Options:
  --filter        Filter tests by name substring
  --reporter      Reporter format (console, junit:path.xml, html:path.html, trx:path.trx) [default: console]
  --workers       Number of parallel workers (or 'auto') [default: auto]
  --visual        Launch visual test runner
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Discover and execute tests from assemblies

Usage:
  Motus.Cli run [<assemblies>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <assemblies>  Test assembly paths

Options:
  --filter        Filter tests by name substring
  --reporter      Reporter format (console, junit:path.xml, html:path.html, trx:path.trx) [default: console]
  --workers       Number of parallel workers (or 'auto') [default: auto]
  --visual        Launch visual test runner
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Capture a screenshot of a web page

Usage:
  Motus.Cli screenshot <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  URL to capture

Options:
  --output        Output file path [default: screenshot.png]
  --full-page     Capture the full scrollable page
  --width         Viewport width in pixels [default: 1280]
  --height        Viewport height in pixels [default: 720]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Trace management commands

Usage:
  Motus.Cli trace [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  show <file>  Display a recorded trace in the visual runner

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display a recorded trace in the visual runner

Usage:
  Motus.Cli trace show <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  Path to a trace ZIP file

Options:
  --port          Port for the trace viewer [default: 5200]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download and update CDP protocol definitions

Usage:
  Motus.Cli update-protocol [options]

Options:
  --version       Protocol version (default: latest)
  --dry-run       Show diff without writing files
  --output-dir    Directory to write protocol files [default: .]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Download and update CDP protocol definitions

Usage:
  Motus.Cli update-protocol [options]

Options:
  --version       Protocol version (default: latest)
  --dry-run       Show diff without writing files
  --output-dir    Directory to write protocol files [default: .]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
